description: In shell, the target shell and the coreutils flavor (GNU vs BSD vs busybox) are part of the contract and changing them changes behavior.
body: In shell, the target shell and the coreutils flavor (GNU vs BSD vs busybox) define how code compiles, lints, and resolves dependencies; bumping any of them can change diagnostics and emitted output. Use when you touch the target shell and the coreutils flavor (GNU vs BSD vs busybox) or when a build behaves differently than the pinned environment. Do not silently upgrade the toolchain or override the pinned version to make an error disappear. Actions: read the pinned config, match the project's the target shell and the coreutils flavor (GNU vs BSD vs busybox), then run shellcheck warnings (especially SC2086 word-splitting and SC2046) and the script under its declared shell (#!/usr/bin/env bash vs POSIX sh) to compare against the baseline. Validation signals include identical shellcheck warnings (especially SC2086 word-splitting and SC2046) output, a reproducible build, and unchanged lockfile resolution. Risk boundary: a toolchain bump is a deliberate, reviewed change; escalate rather than upgrading to unblock yourself.
